title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jiles Reed and Gwendolyn prepare to visit J.J. Avery, the owner of the Water Lily Carriage Company, after Miss Marple warns them that he is a shrewd man. While preparing to leave for Exeter, Jiles receives a call from Dr. Kennedy regarding a strange letter from a woman named Lily Kimball. Dr. Kennedy explains that Lily, a former maid in his house, sent a letter containing a clipping and expressing doubts about a past event involving a woman named Mrs. Harriet. Lily mentions that she saw a fancy car outside and suspects Mr. Kimball might be involved, but she fears police involvement. Dr. Kennedy arranges for Lily to visit on a Thursday so they can all speak together.

During the conversation, Dr. Kennedy reveals that he has heard the name Avery before. He recalls that Jackie Avery was once fired from a firm for attempting to steal confidential information to sell for money. Kennedy notes that Avery showed signs of persecution delusion and emotional instability following his dismissal.

Jiles and Gwendolyn proceed to meet J.J. Avery at his luxurious new home. Avery is described as a plump, middle-aged man with shrewd black eyes. After Jiles discusses a potential business trip for a boys' club, Avery questions why they wish to meet privately. Jiles explains that Gwendolyn wants to reconnect with her stepmother, Helen Harley (formerly Helen Kennedy). Upon hearing the name, Avery reacts with sudden excitement, claiming to remember "Little Miss Helen Kennedy" from eighteen years ago, though he states he has had no news of her since.
